chief_justice_synthesis.notes: ChiefJustice is just another LLM prompt that averages the three judge scores. No hardcoded rules. No dissent summary. Output is console text or unstructured.

git_forensic_analysis.notes: Single 'init' commit or bulk upload of all code at once. No iterative development visible. Timestamps clustered within minutes.

graph_orchestration.notes: Purely linear flow (RepoInvestigator -> DocAnalyst -> Judge -> End). No parallel branches. No synchronization node. No conditional edges for error handling.

judicial_nuance.notes: Single agent acts as 'The Grader' with no persona separation. Or three judges exist but share 90% of prompt text, producing near-identical outputs. Scores are random or purely praise/criticism without nuance.

report_accuracy.notes: Report references files that do not exist. Claims parallel execution but code shows linear flow. Multiple hallucinated paths detected.

safe_tool_engineering.notes: Raw 'os.system("git clone <url>")' drops code into the live working directory. No error handling around shell commands. No input sanitization on the repo URL.

state_management_rigor.notes: Plain Python dicts used for state. No Pydantic models. No reducers, meaning parallel agents will overwrite each other's data.

structured_output_enforcement.notes: Judge nodes call LLMs with plain prompts and parse freeform text responses. No Pydantic validation on output. No retry on parse failure.

swarm_visual.notes:
Generic box-and-arrow diagram with no indication of parallelism. Or no diagram present at all. Diagram shows linear flow that contradicts the parallel architecture claimed in the report.
